207123.explanation: Residents deserve the final say on major decisions affecting their living environment. HvM wants binding referenda and village polls. This truly puts decision-making power with the people, and the municipality decides with you, not about you.

207125.explanation: HvM gives entrepreneurs room to grow. We prevent companies and jobs from moving to other regions. This ensures employment close to home and a strong economy, also for the youth's future in your village.

207126.explanation: West Friesland is the food barn of the region and must remain so. HvM does not sacrifice fertile agricultural land. We see farmers as the best custodians of greenery. This keeps our food local and preserves the polder's open character.

207128.explanation: Young people sometimes wait 12 years for a home. Your future then stalls. HvM finds this unacceptable. Therefore: no more priority for status holders. Everyone waits in line fairly.

207130.explanation: A tidy neighborhood starts with proper maintenance. HvM wants to eliminate neglected greenery. More frequent mowing ensures a neat street image and safety for playing children. Hart voor Medemblik makes sure your village or neighborhood looks spick and span again.

207132.explanation: In a water-rich municipality, swimming lessons are vital. HvM wants every child to learn to swim through school. This way, your child is safe in the water, even if private lessons are too expensive for you. Swimming skills are a basic right.

207133.explanation: Your child deserves a healthy and safe place to learn. HvM therefore implements the school construction plan quickly and fully. The money is reserved for this and must really go to the buildings. No more delays.

207134.explanation: Everything under one roof is convenient for you and familiar for your child. A building where education, childcare, and sports or culture come together forms the beating heart of the neighborhood or village. Bundling forces is the way to keep village schools open. This keeps the village vibrant.

207135.explanation: Safety is a basic right. HvM wants more visible community officers on the street. They address people's behavior and intervene in nuisance situations. This ensures you feel safe everywhere in our municipality and keeps the neighborhood livable.

207136.explanation: HvM supports safety but not unnecessary cameras everywhere. Camera surveillance is a serious measure. We only deploy it where there is actual nuisance. This way, we tackle problems without unnecessarily violating your privacy.

207137.explanation: Domestic violence must stop. HvM believes victims deserve immediate and local shelter. A safe haven in the region is necessary for women and children in need. This offers protection when home is no longer safe.

207138.explanation: There is a great shortage of affordable housing. With sufficient social rental homes, starters and elderly have the chance to stay in their own village. Priority for local residents is important in this regard.

207139.explanation: Our villages have their own atmosphere. Apartments can fit there, also with a view to cheaper housing. HvM chooses homes that match the scale of your village. This keeps your environment recognizable and pleasant to live in.

207141.explanation: A benefit is a safety net, not a hammock. HvM believes you should give something back to the community. That keeps you active, provides rhythm, and increases your chances of a real job. Participating is better than sitting at home alone.

207143.explanation: HvM does not believe in free money. Working must pay off and contributing to society is the norm. We prefer to use your tax money for neighborhood facilities rather than expensive experiments. The safety net is only for those who truly cannot manage.

207146.explanation: No one should stay home due to lack of money. HvM supports practical help through community giveaway cabinets. Accessible and without shame. We help our residents who are struggling financially so that women and girls can continue to participate normally.

207147.explanation: The town hall is too far away for many. HvM brings the municipality to you with service points in every village. For a passport, driver's license, or questions, you no longer have to travel far. We choose convenience, personal contact, and service around the corner.

207148.explanation: HvM believes every resident should have influence, not just a selected group. We choose referenda and village surveys where everyone can join the conversation. Democracy belongs to all of us, not just a few people.

207149.explanation: €2.5 million for, among other things, a luxury council chamber is unnecessary. HvM prefers to lower property taxes or invest in your village. Our tax money is not meant for political perks but for the residents.

207151.explanation: Starting entrepreneurs deserve a flying start. HvM therefore offers vouchers for coaching. This way you can successfully build your business and together we create new jobs locally. We like to give local talent that extra push.

207152.explanation: This is a task for the COA and the national government. HvM prefers to spend municipal money and time on all residents. To help young and old find jobs. We prioritize support for all residents who need a helping hand.

207154.explanation: Housing on the farm means fewer migrant workers in regular houses. HvM chooses this so that homes in your village remain available for you and starters. This solves housing shortages and gives entrepreneurs space.

207155.explanation: Centralized housing ensures regular homes become available again for local residents. Too many houses are currently occupied by migrant workers. HvM chooses this solution so that you have a chance again at an affordable home in your own neighborhood.

207156.explanation: Paying per bag causes illegal dumping and others putting their trash in your bin. HvM wants none of that hassle. A fixed amount keeps your neighborhood clean and is fair for families with young children and people with medical waste.

207157.explanation: The IJsselmeer is nature, not an energy plant for the Randstad. HvM pulls the plug on the mega project for 2.5 million solar panels. We protect our open water and the view. No 'black sea' of solar panels off the coast, but space for recreation and nature.

207158.explanation: Your garden is private. HvM believes the municipality should not interfere. We prefer to spend tax money on good maintenance of public greenery and low charges for everyone rather than on subsidies for your garden.

207159.explanation: HvM has not yet taken a definitive position on this.

207160.explanation: Rigid rules make your new home unnecessarily expensive or delay construction. HvM assesses what suits each neighborhood. Housing comes first: affordable homes for you rather than mandatory percentages on paper.

207161.explanation: Farmers are the caretakers of our landscape. HvM gladly entrusts nature management to them. They know the land like the back of their hand. That works better than expensive external organizations. This keeps the polder beautiful and supports our local farmers.

207162.explanation: Energy storage is a task for energy companies and the market, not the municipality. HvM prefers to invest tax money in core tasks such as good roads, care, and safety in your village rather than in costly experiments.

207164.explanation: Language is the foundation for participation. HvM supports free lessons in the library and community center. This helps eliminate illiteracy so you can manage your affairs independently. No one should be left out because they do not speak the language.

207166.explanation: Large events bring atmosphere and customers to your local entrepreneur. HvM is happy to invest as long as it is safe and genuinely benefits the village or city. A vibrant municipality is good for all of us.

207167.explanation: A funfair is a folk festival. Hart voor Medemblik does not want extra controlling enforcement that puts the funfair's atmosphere under pressure. You don't reach young people with more controls but with clear information and prevention. Therefore, we prefer an informative approach through schools, together with parents, youth work, and the hospitality sector. This prevents problems before they arise instead of having to act afterwards.

207172.explanation: Rules about appearance delay construction. HvM wants less bureaucracy and more speed. We choose low-regulation building. This way you get your permit faster and can start working on your house immediately.

207173.explanation: We tackle energy poverty with insulation. HvM launches a smart fund that allows low-income households to insulate their homes. This lowers your energy bill and leaves you with money to spare. This keeps you warm and housing costs affordable.

207174.explanation: Everyone has talent and deserves a chance at work. HvM removes barriers for entrepreneurs with wage cost subsidies. This way we help people on benefits to find work. Work provides self-esteem and ensures you participate again in society.

207175.explanation: Speeding in residential areas is dangerous. HvM wants speed cameras but only at real high-risk locations. This is about the safety of our children, not about filling the state coffers. We tackle hotspots so everyone is safe.

207176.explanation: Just putting up a sign doesn’t work; that is false security. The road must actually be designed accordingly. HvM wants you to cycle safely. Therefore, speeds only come down when the street is actually adapted for it.

207177.explanation: HvM chooses physical road adaptations near schools. This way the street enforces the speed and your child can cross safely. A sign alone is false security. The road must be designed so that you naturally drive slowly.

207180.explanation: Help must be nearby and accessible. HvM wants social teams in the neighborhood to prevent problems from escalating. No anonymous counters far away in Wognum, but familiar faces in your own village who help you immediately when things get tough.

207181.explanation: Vacancy is bad. HvM intervenes when necessary to preserve a GP or community center in a village. We strategically buy properties to save facilities in your village. This keeps care close and gives you a place to meet each other.

207182.explanation: HvM has fought hard for this. Elderly people deserve a place nearby for all their health questions. Accessible and close by. This prevents problems and ensures you can continue living independently and comfortably at home longer.

207123.explanation: A referendum is too black and white. We believe efforts should focus on more influence and input from residents but in ways that gather more than just for or against views.

207125.explanation: New sites, with respect for the landscape, and maintaining vitality of existing locations are necessary to make our economy future-proof.

207126.explanation: Agricultural land is an economic pillar for our municipality, designated for our food supply. We must be careful with it. Possibly, adjacent plots to existing nature areas could be considered for discussion.

207128.explanation: People with a residence permit (status holders) are otherwise at a disadvantage; they can only register for social housing once they receive their permit. This also prevents asylum seeker centers from becoming overcrowded.

207130.explanation: Mowing more often hinders balanced biodiversity and is more expensive. However, mowing should be more frequent at intersections for traffic safety.

207132.explanation: Responsibility for swimming skills should not be passed to schools. The municipality supports parents who cannot afford swimming lessons.

207133.explanation: We have achieved this in the past four years. It is so important that we have reserved a lot of money for it.

207134.explanation: This has been the goal for some time and is a decision by school boards.

207135.explanation: This has long been our aim and the municipality is already working on it.

207136.explanation: This should not be imposed by the municipality. Entrepreneurs (associations) can submit a request if desired and engage in dialogue.

207137.explanation: It is important to create safety for women in difficult times. We also support this in cooperation with regional municipalities.

207138.explanation: Especially now that many people cannot buy a home, we have the responsibility to ensure sufficient rental housing.

207139.explanation: This choice strongly depends on the location and whether it is urban-planning feasible. If we have to build a lot, sometimes we will also have to build upwards.

207141.explanation: Motivate people to participate in training or volunteer work, but not enforce it.

207143.explanation: A basic income is neither solidaristic nor fair. The CDA is positive about a basic job, which also fits within the existing target group register / labor agreement.

207146.explanation: In favor, but through facilitating private initiatives such as a giveaway cabinet. This can possibly be subsidized through the Activities and Neighborhood Budget.

207147.explanation: This would cost the municipality too much money; special equipment is needed for applications, for example. People can also have a passport delivered at home, for instance.

207148.explanation: This is a good way to shape participation. Thinking together with residents about the future of the municipality.

207149.explanation: Investing in the future of the town hall is urgently needed. All the installations of the town hall are outdated and no longer repairable.

207151.explanation: Help is provided and social assistance is offered. We consider that sufficient.

207152.explanation: This already happens in cooperation with Werksaam, and we are happy to continue that.

207154.explanation: This brings the number in line with neighboring municipality Drechterland and frees up housing in the centers.

207155.explanation: Two in our municipality (Zegersweel and Appelschenkbos) are sufficient. Now it is the neighboring municipalities' turn to take responsibility.

207156.explanation: It is precisely good that there is a financial incentive per household to produce as little residual waste as possible, because that is the most expensive to process/incinerate.

207157.explanation: The CDA has always been in favor of this, but the national government has rejected it twice. Don't keep beating a dead horse. Solar on water can be done, but on a small scale. Think of the solar islands at PWN in Andijk.

207158.explanation: Do not give money, but do stimulate, such as with the tile removal campaign.

207159.explanation: However, the regulations in our municipality may not become stricter than the national regulations.

207160.explanation: CDA is satisfied with the current regulations in this area, namely 3 - 30 - 300. Everyone should be able to see 3 mature trees from home, the neighborhood should have 30% canopy cover, and there must be a green park within 300 meters.

207161.explanation: The combination of landscape managers and farmers works well. Collaborating with the Agricultural Nature Association promotes biodiversity and awareness.

207162.explanation: Regarding investments in energy and climate, our municipality spends the money we receive from the national government. The municipality can, as far as legally possible, take a cooperative approach in areas such as environmental permits.

207164.explanation: Low literacy causes loneliness and insecurity. It is important that people can participate.

207166.explanation: This should not be the approach: but organizations may always submit a subsidy application to the municipality.

207167.explanation: Currently, there is engagement through youth work (L!nk) including education/good conversations, which proves effective. BOA’s only as a supplement if necessary.

207172.explanation: Building is also for the generation that comes after us. Regulations remain important, also in view of historical awareness.

207173.explanation: Possibly via coupons per household in combination with education.

207174.explanation: Do not give money directly to companies. Our current regulations with assistance, knowledge, and subsidies work well (Werksaam, national government, RPA, target group register).

207175.explanation: This option already exists and we are happy to make use of it.

207176.explanation: The road must be safe, but this must be assessed per situation. This should therefore not be everywhere. Road design is more important than a sign.

207177.explanation: A maximum of 30 km/h near schools. This should be reviewed per situation. Road design is more important than a sign.

207180.explanation: This already exists and we are happy to continue it.

207181.explanation: Assess case by case. Make it possible through, for example, permits, but do not buy property outright. Help and facilitate general practitioners but do not necessarily buy a space.

207182.explanation: Currently, general practitioners fulfill this role. We believe it is better to support the general practitioners in this.

207123.explanation: Strong local democracy, in which the voice of the resident counts through participation as part of the municipal participation policy. We therefore focus on participation in advance.

207125.explanation: Create an attractive business climate through (re)development/enlargement of industrial sites and by the presence of good infrastructure. No more industrial estates, but expansion where possible!

207126.explanation: This is not an issue in our municipality; should a farmer have this initiative, it deserves the municipality's cooperation.

207128.explanation: Although this group of people have a different start, do not give total priority (except if an urgency scheme applies).

207130.explanation: Mowing grass in the neighborhoods is done weekly during the growing season. Mowing of roadside edges only after the breeding season. We want green centers and more effort on weed control.

207132.explanation: The goal should be to put more effort into keeping swimming lessons affordable for everyone and helping those who need financial assistance (for example through the youth sports fund). Using an indoor swimming pool in the municipality causes a lot of lost school time due to travel time.

207133.explanation: There is already considerable work on this and it is an important priority. A healthy, safe learning environment is necessary, so investing in future-proof buildings, safe school routes, and sustainable facilities is self-evident.

207134.explanation: This is our preference; however, the highest priority is that there is a school in every village center and/or that it remains. Therefore, the word 'must' does not fit.

207135.explanation: Increase safety through prevention and enforcement visibility (more neighborhood watch, more BOA’s (on land and water), deployment of police and emergency services).

207136.explanation: No additional camera surveillance, except in cases of necessity with safeguards. Camera surveillance can provide good preventive effect. It must be proportionate and in consultation with, among others, entrepreneurs.

207137.explanation: Violence against women is unacceptable and must be tackled firmly, with strong action against femicide. It is good that these places exist; however, are we as a municipality large enough for this? Further investigation is necessary.

207138.explanation: This is in accordance with the council proposal which was adopted by a large majority. Tenants deserve a place in our new construction plans.

207139.explanation: Housing is a basic need: pleasant and affordable living in one's own environment with priority given to youth, seniors, and social housing. In other words: when space is limited, high-rise buildings are a good solution.

207141.explanation: The scheme is executed by WerkSaam. WerkSaam knows which resources need to be deployed to actually activate and develop people.

207143.explanation: Basic income is a national issue and if we want to implement it, it should be done in consultation with the region and therefore also with WerkSaam.

207146.explanation: Access to care prevents/limits neglect and promotes an active and healthy life. There are more resources to mention, and if the municipality is going to implement this, it should be done through an already existing organized network, such as the food bank.

207147.explanation: As beautiful as the idea is, it is not feasible and not affordable. We should focus on enabling digital applications and home delivery.

207148.explanation: Citizens' assemblies are a good means to involve residents in decisions as a form of participation and consultation, but not through lottery. Furthermore, setting up by lottery is also costly.

207149.explanation: GemeenteBelangen supported retaining the current council chamber. We could have saved 1 million euros here, but there was no support for this proposal.

207151.explanation: We stimulate economic developments where (local) entrepreneurs get space to operate. Therefore, no funds, but support at the Entrepreneurs' Desk. Additionally, the Chamber of Commerce can also offer sufficient opportunities.

207152.explanation: We are in favor; this way you also give this group of people meaningful activities. Possibly with help from WerkSaam and the Employers Service Point!

207154.explanation: Agreed, provided it is for our own employees (working at their own company). After all, good childcare and housing for migrant workers is a complement to the local labor market.

207155.explanation: As a municipality, we have contributed our share in the region. Now it is up to the other municipalities in Westfriesland.

207156.explanation: The current policy regarding waste separation is the cheapest and most sustainable: less waste, more reuse, and more space for circular initiatives. We support a fair and transparent tariff.

207157.explanation: Solar panels where responsible and allowed. However, the national government has decided not to proceed with this solar field; further efforts would therefore be unnecessary.

207158.explanation: Money is not the solution. The municipality must raise residents' awareness of climate change. More greenery keeps the living environment healthy.

207159.explanation: In accordance with national regulations, we are responsible for a sustainable living environment. Among other things, by reducing the use of chemical pesticides and properly managing our nature reserves. However, with focus on weed control.

207160.explanation: Residential neighborhoods must be livable; greenery and water are part of that. They also provide nice play areas for children, also in places without playgrounds.

207161.explanation: This belongs with the municipality or the recreational authority.

207162.explanation: This can be a means to accelerate housing construction. Also as support for sustainable initiatives as part of an energy mix.

207164.explanation: Combatting low literacy must be addressed and is a spearhead for GemeenteBelangen. If we can contribute extra to this, we will.

207166.explanation: Such an event must be in the interest of all residents and therefore have value for our municipality. However, a contribution can possibly be made within existing subsidies; if entrepreneurs benefit, they may be willing to co-finance.

207167.explanation: This is now also part of the tasks of the BOAs (special enforcement officers), who are extra vigilant at events like fairs. Increasing safety through prevention and visible enforcement.

207172.explanation: Completely abolishing it goes too far, but the influence of the aesthetic committee may be reduced.

207173.explanation: This is already current policy; keep doing what works well, as far as GemeenteBelangen is concerned.

207174.explanation: In cooperation with WerkSaam. Also, the municipality can better promote existing opportunities and set a good example itself.

207175.explanation: Speed cameras ensure people drive slower, which increases traffic safety. Preferably flexible cameras so they can be used more broadly.

207176.explanation: We must protect vulnerable road users as much as possible by applying a 30 km/h limit on roads with mixed fast and slow traffic; use a 50 km/h limit only where there is a separated cycle path and public transport.

207177.explanation: Extra attention to traffic safety around children (schools, playgrounds) and the elderly. So not just with a sign, preferably also adapt the layout so that everyone can see they are in a school zone.

207180.explanation: Every resident deserves care, support, and opportunities to participate fully, with visible neighborhood teams as a means. Practically, merging certain village centers as a work area can be an option.

207181.explanation: The presence of good care facilities is a basic need in or near the neighborhood. This prevents problems in the near future. However, the municipality should not behave like real estate developers.

207182.explanation: As GemeenteBelangen, we do not find this directly necessary. There are general practitioners and home care, as well as other institutions where the elderly can go. If there is an opportunity, it must be carefully considered.

207123.explanation: The VVD supports participation. An advisory referendum can be an option, but only for a very important (municipality-wide) issue. We do not want to waste money. Besides, there are various restrictions on referenda, so it remains an exceptional situation.

207125.explanation: The VVD supports entrepreneurs wherever possible. Our preference is for renewal of existing industrial estates. But new locations must be possible if there is demand.

207126.explanation: The VVD supports farmers and wants to preserve agricultural land. Therefore, we oppose the mandatory conversion of agricultural land into nature. Of course, if an entrepreneur wants to do so, that is allowed.

207128.explanation: There is a severe housing shortage in our municipality. Therefore, the VVD opposes giving priority to refugees for social rental housing. All vulnerable groups must be treated equally.

207130.explanation: The VVD wants streets and parks in the municipality to look neat. That is not currently the case. Therefore, grass must be mowed more often.

207132.explanation: Parents are responsible for teaching their children to swim. Schools should not be burdened with more tasks beyond education. Support may be possible for those with fewer means.

207133.explanation: The VVD supports the current housing plan for all schools. It specifies when each school will be improved. Some schools have been recently renovated. There's no automatic need for more funding.

207134.explanation: It is good if schools, childcare, and other institutions collaborate. If they want to be in one building, we welcome that. But it is not a requirement that the municipality should enforce. That leads to unnecessary bureaucracy.

207135.explanation: The VVD wants a safer municipality. Unfortunately, the national government provides too few police officers. Therefore, we support the deployment of BOAs (special enforcement officers).

207136.explanation: The VVD wants a safer municipality. Camera surveillance helps with that. Not only in shopping centers but also in other locations with increased risk.

207137.explanation: The VVD is fine with establishing a women's shelter. But we believe the municipality should not have to start that itself.

207138.explanation: The VVD wants to build for all target groups, but at least 35% social housing is too much for us. We prefer more affordable owner-occupied homes. That way, more young people get a chance to buy a house.

207139.explanation: The VVD wants to build more houses and therefore supports residential apartment buildings. Of course, suitable locations and good integration into the environment must be carefully considered.

207141.explanation: The VVD supports a social contribution requirement for people with social assistance benefits. That way, everyone contributes their part.

207143.explanation: The VVD believes work is the best income. Benefits are intended as a safety net for those who cannot (temporarily) work. We are against a basic income.

207146.explanation: The VVD believes distributing tampons and sanitary pads is not a task for the municipality. However, support for vulnerable groups can be considered, for example through food banks.

207147.explanation: The VVD values service for residents. But it is not necessary to have a municipal desk in every district. That costs too much taxpayer money. We prefer to invest in digitization and the (safe) delivery of identity cards and driver’s licenses.

207148.explanation: The VVD finds residents’ opinions very important. But there is no need to select a group of residents by lot for this purpose. Anyone can advise or consult the municipal council if they wish.

207149.explanation: The VVD believes the renovation of the town hall can be cheaper. That is why we did not agree with relocating the council chamber and building an expensive corridor between two buildings. That should be reversed.

207151.explanation: The VVD wants to encourage entrepreneurship. Entrepreneurs know best how to run their businesses. The municipality does not need to coach them. The municipality can better help by providing services and cutting regulations.

207152.explanation: The VVD wants asylum seekers to integrate quickly. Work is the best benefit. Therefore, we believe the municipality should help with finding jobs.

207154.explanation: The VVD supports housing foreign workers at agricultural companies. We have also submitted a plan to increase the maximum number to 120. This leaves more regular homes available for local residents.

207155.explanation: The municipality already has two large residential complexes. That is enough for now. The VVD believes it is important that other West Frisian municipalities also do their best for this type of housing.

207156.explanation: The VVD supports the current system for residual waste. Households pay a fixed amount for waste collection charges. Those who offer residual waste pay a separate fee per collection. The fewer times the grey residual waste bin is set out, the less you pay. We consider this a fair system.

207157.explanation: The VVD does not want solar panels on the IJsselmeer. Therefore, we oppose this plan. Fortunately, the project has already been cancelled at the national level. If the plan returns, we will remain opposed.

207158.explanation: The VVD welcomes when people replace tiles in their garden with soil. But that is their own choice. We do not believe the municipality should pay for this.

207159.explanation: The VVD wants public green spaces to look neat and clean. Some weed control agents (such as Roundup) are legally banned for municipalities. But agents that are not banned may, in the VVD’s opinion, be used. Also consider creative solutions.

207160.explanation: The VVD is against unnecessary rules. Therefore, we do not want mandatory minimum percentages for water and greenery in new neighborhoods. That slows down housing construction. If a project developer wants to beautify the neighborhoods, that is, of course, fine. But the municipality should not make it compulsory.

207161.explanation: The VVD supports farmers; with their own land, they manage most of the green space. But for nature reserves, we think management is better in the hands of specialized organizations (such as Landschap Noord-Holland).

207162.explanation: The VVD believes in personal responsibility. If a neighborhood wants to buy a battery on its own initiative, we think that is fine. But the municipality does not have to contribute financially. However, the municipality should be flexible in granting permits.

207164.explanation: There are already initiatives to reduce illiteracy. The VVD supports that. But it is unnecessary to allocate more funds than are currently being allocated.

207166.explanation: The VVD believes in personal responsibility and entrepreneurship. Commercial events are not a municipal task. However, the municipality can grant subsidies to non-profit events. This is already happening, and we support it.

207167.explanation: The VVD advocates enforcement against the use of prohibited substances, including drugs. Alcohol use is allowed unless it involves breaking the law (such as serving minors). There is never enough police to enforce well, so we propose deploying municipal BOAs.

207172.explanation: The VVD wants fewer rules to enable more and faster building. We support building without aesthetic regulations and assume people will build attractive homes.

207173.explanation: The VVD believes in personal responsibility. Providing insulation material free of charge is not a municipal task.

207174.explanation: The VVD believes that people with disabilities should have employment opportunities. The municipality already provides subsidies for this, which we support. It is also important to look at removing barriers for entrepreneurs.

207175.explanation: The VVD wants to improve traffic safety and increase enforcement. There is currently a lot of nuisance from speeding drivers. Speed cameras within built-up areas help combat this.

207176.explanation: The VVD wants safe roads but also good traffic flow. In some places, 30 km/h may be an improvement. But we do not think this should apply automatically everywhere. The VVD wants tailor-made solutions.

207177.explanation: Some schools already have speed limits. In busy locations, this can work well. The VVD supports that. But it does not have to apply automatically at every school. The situation differs per school.

207180.explanation: The VVD believes in personal responsibility. The municipality can assist people who need support. But it is not necessary to set up something in every village. It must be demand-driven and effective.

207181.explanation: The VVD believes in initiatives from residents themselves. We do not think the municipality should set up general practitioner practices or community centers itself. However, the municipality can support with subsidies and facilitate by granting permits quickly and flexibly.

207182.explanation: We see that there are more seniors every year. A local health center can support them. But preferably in combination with other (existing) facilities. The VVD supports preventive care.

207123.explanation: MORGEN! wants to involve residents in decision-making. A referendum is too limited an instrument for this, since you can only vote yes or no. MORGEN! advocates for a citizens' assembly or a citizen panel, where residents can genuinely think along and participate!

207125.explanation: MORGEN! wants to allow space for businesses as well as housing and greenery. Therefore, first better utilize existing business parks and expand them on a limited scale, provided these companies offer a sustainable addition to our municipality and labor market. In short: no new distribution centers.

207126.explanation: MORGEN! wants more greenery and trees in the municipality of Medemblik. Additionally, our municipality’s population will grow in the coming years. MORGEN! therefore wants to create more space for nature and recreation, of course in coordination with the agricultural sector.

207128.explanation: MORGEN! believes that status holders should neither have higher nor lower chances than other residents. However, the municipality must continue to meet its legal obligations. Priority in social housing remains necessary to help people with a residence permit start quickly and well.

207130.explanation: MORGEN! does not think public grass should be mowed more frequently everywhere. The management is in order. Where possible, mow less to strengthen biodiversity; only in special cases, based on safety or intensive use, should mowing be more frequent.

207132.explanation: MORGEN! finds it important that all children learn to swim. Extra municipal funds for school swimming are not necessary. There are already schemes for low-income families to ensure swimming lessons remain accessible to everyone.

207133.explanation: MORGEN! considers it very important that schools are well maintained. Fortunately, this is done through the multi-year maintenance plan. In short: not all schools within 4 years. In recent years, significant investments have been made in our school buildings, and MORGEN! will continue this.

207134.explanation: MORGEN! sees advantages in having one building for education, childcare and after-school activities and wants to stimulate this. But it should not become a requirement. Parents must maintain the freedom to move or choose another school if that suits their child better.

207135.explanation: MORGEN! wants everyone to feel safe in the municipality of Medemblik. More investments have already been made in recent years, but as far as MORGEN! is concerned, even more can be done.

207136.explanation: MORGEN! finds safety very important and believes that in case of increased crime, proportional measures to allow the installation of cameras should be possible. At the same time, MORGEN! does not want camera surveillance to become the standard everywhere: people should not be watched everywhere.

207137.explanation: MORGEN! considers it important that there is sufficient support for victims of domestic violence. A women's shelter is necessary but should be organized regionally to ensure safety, expertise, and continuity are well guaranteed.

207138.explanation: MORGEN! agrees with a minimum of 35% social rental housing in new construction. The shortage of affordable rental housing is large. Through the housing vision, MORGEN! aims for 35% social rental and at least 35% affordable owner-occupied housing, so up to 70% of new homes are affordable for residents.

207139.explanation: MORGEN! finds it important to tackle the housing shortage. In suitable and well-integrated locations, residential buildings with more than four floors can contribute to more homes without compromising liveability or spatial quality.

207141.explanation: MORGEN! does not consider it right to require extra social work alongside the existing labor obligation in social assistance. Participation is important but must always be tailored and aligned with an individual's capabilities, not through a general obligation.

207143.explanation: MORGEN! is not in favor of a trial with a basic income in Medemblik. Experimenting can be valuable, but the party prefers to leave this to other municipalities or the national government. As far as MORGEN! is concerned, Medemblik focuses on activation and customized solutions within existing policy.

207146.explanation: MORGEN! believes that menstrual products should be available to those who need them. Distribution points should be created at schools and community centers. Targeted support through poverty policy and facilities is more effective and fairer than a general free scheme.

207147.explanation: MORGEN! thinks it is important that municipal services remain easily accessible, but a service desk in every village is too costly and unrealistic. MORGEN! advocates for civil servants to be more visible in the villages, for example by holding consultations or working in village halls.

207148.explanation: MORGEN! wants to involve residents in decision-making. A so-called citizens' assembly composed by lottery on certain topics can help with this and has already produced good results in various places in the country.

207149.explanation: The town hall no longer met occupational health and sustainability requirements. After more than 20 years without investments, renovation was necessary. The current plan focuses on safety, sustainability, and functionality and is deliberately designed to be sober and efficient.

207151.explanation: MORGEN! believes that funding coaching and training for entrepreneurs is not a task for the municipality. Entrepreneurship requires personal initiative; the municipality focuses on good conditions for a strong local business climate.

207152.explanation: MORGEN! finds that asylum seekers should participate in society as quickly as possible. Paid work helps with integration, language development, and independence and is good for them as well as for society as a whole.

207154.explanation: MORGEN! disagrees with raising the maximum to 120 foreign workers on one site. Housing for labor migrants must remain small-scale and manageable. A maximum of 60 ensures better integration and liveability in the surrounding area.

207155.explanation: MORGEN! opposes more large residential complexes for foreign workers. Two locations in Wognum and Wervershoof are, in MORGEN!'s view, already more than enough. The party aims for an economy where labor migrants are not structurally needed.

207156.explanation: MORGEN! is against a fixed fee for residual waste and supports the recycling fee: those who separate waste pay less. This increases residents' awareness and has made waste collection in Medemblik demonstrably cheaper than in surrounding municipalities.

207157.explanation: MORGEN! believes the municipality should continue to advocate with the national government for a solar field on the IJsselmeer. Solar panels on small islands, combined with nature development, can contribute to sustainable energy and ecological strengthening.

207158.explanation: MORGEN! encourages the greening of gardens. The municipality helps with advice, removal of tiles, and providing plants.

207159.explanation: MORGEN! supports pesticide-free management of public green spaces. The use of pesticides harms people, animals, and nature. By maintaining pesticide-free management, the party protects residents' health and biodiversity in verges and parks.

207160.explanation: MORGEN! supports new neighborhoods with sufficient water and greenery. A share of 30% ensures a pleasant living environment, helps with climate adaptation, and contributes to residents' health and well-being.

207161.explanation: Nature management requires specific knowledge and goals. Farmers can sometimes play a role, but are not automatically suitable. The municipality must decide per area what is best for nature, landscape, and biodiversity.

207162.explanation: Research into neighborhood batteries is not a core task of the municipality, but it is important. Due to grid congestion, new neighborhoods often cannot be connected. Neighborhood batteries can help store locally generated solar power and still supply energy to neighborhoods.

207164.explanation: MORGEN! supports more free reading and writing lessons in libraries and community centers. This low-threshold approach tackles illiteracy and gives people more opportunities to participate in work, care, and society.

207166.explanation: Events can receive support if they fit within existing subsidy policy and contribute to our municipality of Medemblik. Size alone is not a reason for financial support.

207167.explanation: MORGEN! finds supervision of alcohol and drug use among young people important. The current policy and existing enforcement at fairs are sufficient for this. Additional efforts are unnecessary as long as this approach proves effective.

207172.explanation: MORGEN! thinks abolishing rules about building appearances goes too far. Agreements on aesthetic criteria protect the appearance of villages and neighborhoods and contribute to cohesion and liveability. Relaxation is sometimes possible, but supervision remains necessary.

207173.explanation: MORGEN! considers energy poverty among tenants a problem and finds it important to encourage housing associations to insulate their homes. Existing schemes also help people with owner-occupied homes to insulate their houses.

207174.explanation: MORGEN! finds it important that there are schemes to help people with disabilities find work. The existing instruments are sufficient. It is questionable whether extra subsidies to companies should be a municipality task.

207175.explanation: MORGEN! supports more speed cameras within built-up areas from a safety and liveability perspective. At the same time, MORGEN! recognizes that the Public Prosecution Service often first advises adjusting road design. Both measures can contribute to safer traffic.

207176.explanation: MORGEN! opposes a general 30 km/h rule where cars and bicycles share the road. This is often appropriate within built-up areas, but on many roads outside built-up areas without bike lanes, this is not realistic. Tailored solutions remain necessary there.

207177.explanation: MORGEN! believes children's safety comes first. A maximum speed of 15 km/h near schools increases traffic safety, improves visibility, and reduces the chance of serious accidents around school times.

207180.explanation: MORGEN! finds it important that help with debts, care, and upbringing is close by and easily accessible. Each village should be served by a social team, but this does not necessarily mean a physical team must be established in each village.

207181.explanation: MORGEN! is against the municipality routinely purchasing vacated properties. That is not efficient. In special situations, purchase may be necessary if there is an acute social need and the municipality must temporarily take control. This can also concern, for example, general practices.

207182.explanation: MORGEN! supports health centers where elderly residents can go with questions. It also finds it important that such facilities are accessible to all residents. This strengthens prevention, support, and health for everyone.

207123.explanation: We support maximum participation of residents. A referendum under conditions is one of the instruments for this.

207125.explanation: Businesses are the backbone of our local and regional economy. We must facilitate them wherever possible.

207126.explanation: We must be careful with our agricultural land and green environment. Liveability in our municipality requires tough choices regarding the (re)designation of farmland. Residents benefit from more space for nature and recreation.

207128.explanation: It is necessary to provide assistance to vulnerable people with economic and cultural disadvantages. Also to reduce homelessness and vagrancy.

207130.explanation: We strive for a balance between nuisance and ecology. It is a matter of customization. Green management must remain affordable.

207132.explanation: Drowning is terrible and learning to swim is a necessity. All children must be able to learn to swim. If the municipality can contribute to that, we of course support it!

207133.explanation: Education must take place in buildings that are modern. A healthy learning environment and a sustainable indoor climate. We are already investing heavily in this and will continue to do so.

207134.explanation: A noble goal. We are happy to actively look for opportunities to realize this.

207135.explanation: Safety is paramount. More visible enforcement officers on the street can contribute to a greater sense of security.

207136.explanation: We support that. The content and elaboration must be determined by entrepreneurs and the municipality together.

207137.explanation: Unfortunately, violence against women is a great injustice. We do believe this is an issue where the West Friesland municipalities should stand united. Think of choosing a safe location with good professional support.

207138.explanation: The waiting lists for social rental housing are now much too long and not everyone can or wants to obtain a home to buy. These percentages are not set in stone. They are targets that depend on the market.

207139.explanation: That is certainly possible at certain locations in Medemblik. Those buildings have stood in the city for years. Also, for example in Wognum near the A7 there are tall buildings where more than four floors are possible. It is also necessary to protect the open landscape elsewhere.

207141.explanation: Meaningful work can help people out of a negative spiral and find social connection under certain circumstances.

207143.explanation: This is an important point for us. The complex system of benefits and allowances urgently needs simplification. Currently, people get stuck in procedures that are difficult to understand and the money does not always reach the most vulnerable. If a pilot project for this is launched in Medemblik, we support it.

207146.explanation: Period poverty is a distressing situation that does not belong in our modern, prosperous society.

207147.explanation: That sounds nice but is unaffordable. The frequency of replacing, for example, a passport or driving license is too low for that to be feasible. That cannot be covered. There are other ways to help residents who need this. Also through the municipality.

207148.explanation: Participation and involvement are important. A citizens' council or panel can help with that.

207149.explanation: That has been decided and is therefore a fact. The council has made a final decision with a budget cut of €650,000. This benefits the Medemblik City Plan.

207151.explanation: There are already various schemes to support (starting) entrepreneurs. Entrepreneurs must create a business plan with a view to profitability. That is the essence of entrepreneurship.

207152.explanation: Integration of newcomers benefits from finding (paid) work. The municipality can help reduce economic and cultural differences.

207154.explanation: Living near a business should align with job availability. Especially now the focus is on housing with separation of bed and bread. Robotization should also lead to less dependence on labor migrants to maintain broad prosperity.

207155.explanation: Not necessary because Medemblik has already met its share of the realization. This should also ensure the release of housing and less nuisance.

207156.explanation: We support a well-functioning system for waste separation. The current system works well. Residents can reduce costs by separating waste properly. Separation at the source also provides reusable raw materials. Good for both the wallet and the environment.

207157.explanation: Focusing on sustainable energy is really urgently needed. The IJsselmeer is large and (technological) developments continue. An open mind for opportunities for reliable energy supply remains necessary.

207158.explanation: We support greening gardens. But that remains a choice for residents for their own grounds and gardens. Expanding tasks from public to private space is basically incorrect and leads to extra burden for residents.

207159.explanation: The municipality already no longer uses poisons in municipal green management. Diseases such as Parkinson's are linked to the use of poisons like glyphosate. Eliminating that is really necessary and the municipality must take and maintain leadership in this.

207160.explanation: A correct principle to keep neighborhoods attractive and livable for pleasant living, greenery, heat stress, and climate adaptation.

207161.explanation: Farmers are primarily responsible for managing their land. They already have to do a lot to make it more sustainable in the future. They also need help with that. It can be an option in some respects, but the recreation board is now an excellent partner in this.

207162.explanation: Solving grid congestion is a joint responsibility of grid operators, energy companies, entrepreneurs and households. Neighborhood batteries and other options to generate your own electricity are promising. This serves the public interest. Keep in mind: power outages cost much more!

207164.explanation: Low literacy puts people at a disadvantage and hinders them in daily life. Reading proficiency in our region is unfortunately under pressure. More participation is needed. It yields more than it costs because it contributes to broad prosperity.

207166.explanation: Large entertainment events should primarily be able to operate without tax money. An exception can be made if there is an economic or social interest and it connects with community and pride.

207167.explanation: That is primarily the responsibility of the caregivers. (Sometimes also the municipality's, but not during the fair.)

207172.explanation: Completely abolishing aesthetic regulation inevitably leads to disorder and annoyance. Where possible, the administrative burden should be reduced.

207173.explanation: There are already municipal schemes to help people with sustainability. This can be financial but also with materials. It must reach people who really need support.

207174.explanation: That is certainly an option if it leads to people gaining job prospects and benefits being limited or abolished. Providing support remains necessary to include everyone.

207175.explanation: We are in favor of measures that increase traffic safety within built-up areas. However, we doubt the effectiveness of speed cameras. Adjusting road design is much more important.

207176.explanation: Not everywhere. In rural areas, that is not feasible. In built-up areas, it is a good idea to increase traffic safety.

207177.explanation: Especially at schools with many children who respond spontaneously and can move in all directions, this should be the norm. To prevent accidents and provide children with a safe school and learning environment.

207180.explanation: An integrated social domain system is already available in all villages. This helps residents with problems related to money, care, and upbringing. This is The Social Desk with consultants for advice and support on various social issues.

207181.explanation: The municipality's task is to facilitate the establishment of general practitioners' offices or community centers. We do not see the purchase of properties as a municipal task.

207182.explanation: We are not against it if a good initiative presents itself. Aging is also a concern in our municipality. Our primary focus would be to work optimally with existing care, welfare, and social networks and services.

207125.explanation: The expansion of industrial areas may be desirable, but it is not a necessity. It must be considered on a case-by-case basis, with attention to the surroundings.

207137.explanation: Domestic violence must be addressed seriously. We want to first investigate which approach is most effective. A women's shelter can be an option, but that is not automatic.

207155.explanation: We build first for our own residents, not for newcomers.

207161.explanation: We want the municipality to actively cooperate with farmers on nature management, but the municipality should not completely wash its hands of it.

207166.explanation: The municipality can choose to contribute financially, but this depends on the type of event we want to attract as a municipality. So the rule is: we do not contribute unless it is an event we really want.

statements.title: There should be more camera surveillance in all shopping centers in the municipality of Medemblik.

titleParts.text: In alle winkelcentra van de gemeente Medemblik moet meer

titleParts.glossary: Monitoring of a building, area, or (groups of) people using security cameras.

statements.title: New neighborhoods should be composed of 30 percent water and green space.
Every household should pay the same fee for residual waste, regardless of how much waste they produce.
statements.moreInfo: In the municipality of Medemblik a multi-person household pays the fixed waste collection fee of €315.41. A single-person household pays €237.15. In addition, residents pay each time the municipality empties their container. Emptying a 140-liter residual waste container costs €10.39. Opening an underground container to throw in residual waste costs €3.48 per time.

statements.title: The municipality must allow agricultural land in Medemblik to be converted into nature reserves.
Every village should have a municipal service desk where residents can, for example, apply for a passport.
The municipality must continue to give refugees with a residence permit priority for social housing.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet vluchtelingen met een

titleParts.glossary: The right to reside in the Netherlands. The term can refer to the right itself or to the card that shows someone has that right.
A rental home with a low rent (in 2026 at most €932.93 per month) for people with a low income.

statements.title: The municipality must spend less on renovating the town hall.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet minder uitgeven aan de

titleParts.glossary: Repairing or partially renewing an existing building.

statements.moreInfo: At the beginning of 2025 the municipal council decided to renovate the town hall in Wognum. The municipality estimates the renovation will cost about €18 million. The renovation will begin in 2027.

statements.title: The municipality of Medemblik must establish a women's shelter for victims of domestic violence.

titleParts.text: In de gemeente Medemblik moet een

titleParts.glossary: Shelter for women who experience violence from their partner or family. The shelter is secured; sometimes the location is secret.

statements.title: The municipality should allocate money for school swimming.
statements.moreInfo: School swimming is swimming lessons that students receive through school. The goal is for children to learn to swim and to be able to keep themselves safe in the water. In the past school swimming was offered in many municipalities. Nowadays parents often arrange this themselves, so not all children obtain a swimming diploma. At the moment the municipality of Medemblik provides money to low-income families so children can obtain swimming diplomas A and B.

statements.title: More business parks should be developed in the municipality of Medemblik.
Libraries and community centers should offer more free reading and writing classes.
The municipality should mow public grass more often.
statements.moreInfo: The municipality is responsible for mowing grass in public spaces, such as lawns, parks and verges. The municipality mows the lawns 26 times a year. The verges are mowed twice a year.

statements.title: At least 35% of the newly built homes in the municipality of Medemblik should be social rental homes. In Medemblik now 17% of the new homes are social rental homes.

titleParts.text: Van de nieuw te bouwen woningen in de gemeente Medemblik moet minimaal 35% een

titleParts.glossary: A rental home with a low rent (in 2026 at most €932.93 per month) for people with a low income.

titleParts.text: zijn. In Medemblik is nu 17% van de nieuwe woningen een

titleParts.glossary: A rental home with a low rent (in 2026 at most €932.93 per month) for people with a low income.

statements.title: Residents of the municipality of Medemblik should be able to give their opinion on important issues via a referendum.

titleParts.text: De inwoners van de gemeente Medemblik moeten over belangrijke onderwerpen hun mening kunnen geven via een

titleParts.glossary: A public consultation where residents can express their opinion on a political issue. The municipal council is not obliged to follow the result; legally it cannot.

statements.title: The maximum speed near schools should be 15 km/h.
The municipality must allow agricultural businesses to house up to 120 foreign workers on their own premises instead of 60.
The municipality must continue to advocate with the national government for a solar park on the IJsselmeer.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet zich bij het Rijk blijven inzetten voor een

statements.title: People receiving social assistance should, in return, do socially useful work.

titleParts.glossary: For example cleaning the streets or keeping company and supporting elderly residents.

statements.title: Medemblik should have a health center where elderly people can go with questions.
The municipality should invest in research into neighborhood batteries to store electricity from solar panels.
The municipality should contribute financially when a large event comes to Medemblik.
The municipality should distribute tampons and sanitary pads for free.
The municipality should give companies money to hire more people with disabilities.
The municipality should ask the Public Prosecution Service to install more speed cameras within built-up areas.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet aan het Openbaar Ministerie vragen om meer

titleParts.glossary: Devices along the road that record traffic violations.

titleParts.text: neer te zetten binnen de bebouwde kom.

statements.title: Residents with low incomes must receive free insulation materials for their homes from the municipality.

titleParts.text: Inwoners met weinig geld moeten van de gemeente gratis

titleParts.glossary: Material in roofs, walls and floors that keeps heat in or out. In winter it keeps heat inside and in summer outside.

statements.title: Every village should have a social team to help residents with problems related to money, care, and parenting.
The municipality should abolish municipal rules about how houses must look when being built or renovated.
The municipality should help asylum seekers find paid work.

titleParts.glossary: People who have applied for asylum and are still waiting for a decision.

titleParts.text: helpen met vinden van betaald werk.

statements.title: The municipality may no longer use poison when managing public green spaces, such as verges and parks.
The municipality should deploy more municipal enforcement officers (BOAs) at fairs to check for alcohol and drug use among young people.

titleParts.glossary: People who, for the municipality, check whether citizens and businesses comply with the rules.

titleParts.text: (boa’s) inzetten op kermissen om te controleren op drank- en drugsgebruik onder jongeren.

shootoutStatements.title: The municipality must invest in improving all school buildings over the next four years.
Residential apartment blocks of more than four storeys may be built in the municipality of Medemblik.
More large housing complexes should be built for foreign workers.
The municipality of Medemblik should employ more municipal enforcement officers (BOAs) who focus on public safety.
The municipality of Medemblik should run a trial of a basic income for the long-term unemployed.
The management of natural areas should be left to farmers.
Children should be able to access education, childcare and after-school activities from birth through primary school in a single building.
A group of residents selected by lottery should advise the municipality on important issues.
The municipality should give start-up entrepreneurs funding for coaching and training.
The municipality must give money to people who replace paving in their garden with greenery.
Wherever cyclists and cars share the road, the speed limit should be reduced to 30 km/h.
The municipality should buy vacant properties to house GP practices or community centers.
